data.title: Fairfax expects $500mn-$750mn net loss from LA wildfires
data.description:
(The Insurer) - Fairfax Financial expects $500mn to $750mn of net losses from the Los Angeles wildfires, which will primarily come from reinsurance exposure at Odyssey, Brit and Allied World.

During an investor call on Friday, Fairfax president and chief operating officer Peter Clarke said that the losses from January's wildfires in Southern California will primarily be a reinsurance event for the company because it has “very little” direct insurance exposure to the fires.

“Our main exposure is through the reinsurance business at Odyssey, Brit and Allied World. Insured industry losses are estimated to be in the $35bn to $45bn range, and we typically take on 1 percent to 1.5 percent of the industry losses,” Clarke said.

“This could be a little higher given this is a reinsurance event for us. But at a high level, our early estimate of the net losses would be in the $500mn to $750mn range on a pre-tax basis.”

When asked by an analyst whether the wildfires would change how Toronto-based Fairfax thinks about its catastrophe exposure, Clarke said: “I think the simple answer is no. We're very comfortable where our exposure is on the cat side. Through the hard market, we grew significantly. We didn't grow as much on catastrophe exposure but where we are today we're very comfortable.”

Clarke suggested that “rates might firm up after such a large loss such as this”. He added that Fairfax’s losses are within its expectations and manageable within its underwriting profit.

Fairfax announced on Thursday that its adjusted operating income from P&C insurance and reinsurance operations increased from $1.21bn to $1.53bn in the fourth quarter, and from $3.94bn to $4.76bn for the year.

Gross premiums written at the group level increased by 14 percent in Q4 to $7.55bn. Net premiums written rose 15 percent to $5.92bn.

For the full year, GPW was up 13 percent to $32.83bn, while NPW increased 12 percent to $25.61bn.

Fairfax’s overall P&C (re)insurance combined ratio improved moderately in Q4 from 89.9 percent to 89.5 percent on an undiscounted basis.

The quarter included $399.2mn of cat losses, or 6.4 points on the combined ratio, with Hurricane Milton the largest contributor at $235.4mn, or 3.8 points. This was partially offset by favourable prior year reserve development of $301.4mn, up from $151.7mn in Q4 2023.

On the investor call, Clarke noted that Fairfax’s combined ratio was 92.7 percent for the year and that the company produced record underwriting profit of $1.8bn in 2024.

“The combined ratio included catastrophe losses of $1.1bn, adding 4.5 combined ratio points, primarily from hurricanes Milton and Helene in the United States, the significant catastrophe events in Canada and the floods in Dubai. This compares to a combined ratio of 93.2 percent and catastrophe losses of 4 points in 2023,” he said.

Clarke added: “As our premium base has expanded and with the benefits of diversification, we expect to be able to absorb significant catastrophe losses within our underlying underwriting profit.”

Allied World improved its combined ratio from 86.1 percent in Q4 2023 to 83.4 percent in the final quarter of 2024.

Odyssey Group’s combined ratio improved from 88.1 percent to 85.3 percent in the quarter while Crum & Forster’s dropped from 95.9 percent to 92.6 percent and Northbridge’s improved from 91.5 percent to 83.3 percent.

Brit's Q4 combined ratio deteriorated from 88.3 percent to 97.2 percent. The metric also increased at Zenith National from 87.0 percent to 101.8 percent, and in the firm’s international insurers and reinsurers division, from 93.4 percent to 95.5 percent.

data.title: FBN, MORNINGS WITH MARIA INTERVIEW WITH SCOTT BESSENT, SECRETARY OF TREASURY
data.description:
TRANSCRIPT

February 14, 2025

NEWS PROGRAM

SCOTT BESSENT, SECRETARY OF TREASURY

FBN, MORNINGS WITH MARIA INTERVIEW WITH SCOTT BESSENT,

SECRETARY OF TREASURY

VIQ Media Transcription, Inc.

20 East Thomas Road, Suite 2200

Phoenix, AZ 85012

asc.info@viqsolutions.com

Copyright 2025. Provided under license from VIQ Media Transcription, Inc.

All materials herein are protected by United States copyright law

and/or license from VIQ Media Transcription, Inc., and may not be

reproduced, distributed, transmitted, displayed, published or

broadcast without the prior written permission of

VIQ Media Transcription, Inc.

You may not alter or remove any trademark, copyright or other

notice from copies of the content.

FBN, MORNINGS WITH MARIA INTERVIEW WITH SCOTT BESSENT,

SECRETARY OF TREASURY

FEBRUARY 14, 2025

SPEAKERS:

SCOTT BESSENT, SECRETARY OF TREASURY

MARIA BARTIROMO, FBN ANCHOR

MARIA BARTIROMO, FBN ANCHOR: Joining me now is U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent. Treasury Secretary Bessent, great to have you this morning. Congratulations to you and thank you for joining us.

SCOTT BESSENT, SECRETARY OF TREASURY: Good morning, Maria. Always a pleasure to be with you.

BARTIROMO: Secretary, I'm wondering if some of this is theater. The president said that he was going to impose the reciprocal tariffs a week ago, now yesterday, instead of actually putting them into action, he said you're going to study them. Is he saying that if Europe cuts its tariffs then the United States will cut tariffs and will not impose a reciprocal tariff on Europe?

BESSENT: Well, Maria, as we've learned that with President Trump, you should take him at his word. This is not theater. The April 1st deadline is for a study that the Commerce Department is doing on global tariffs that apply to U.S. products, country-by-country. And also, we're not just looking at tariffs, but we're looking at non-tariff barriers, the local content, things like that. And we're also looking at currency manipulation.

As I repeatedly said, the U.S. has a strong dollar policy, but because we have a strong dollar policy, it doesn't mean that other countries get to have a weak currency policy. So we're going to come up with what is the equivalent of an index, what I would call reciprocal index, country-by-country, the outstanding tariffs, non-tariff, the trade barriers and currency manipulation.

And President Trump is serious about negotiating this. And look, I don't understand why there's any pushback on this, that if they want to bring all these barriers down, then we will have more friction with global trade.

BARTIROMO: So are you expecting the president to institute reciprocal tariffs in April?

BESSENT: I am expecting that we will get this report, and we will see how our trading partners choose to respond, but I can tell you that if he needs to, he will implement them. And as he said in the interview yesterday, this can be a big revenue source for the U.S. government.

BARTIROMO: Yes, and he's talked about the external revenue service, of course, sir. But look, you mentioned the criticism. The Wall Street Journal is out with an op-ed this morning, and here's the title, "reciprocal tariffs make no sense." The author writes, Trump says the U.S. will, quote, impose the same tariffs on other countries as they oppose on — impose on the U.S. No more, no less. That sounds fair. We treat them the way they treat us. But it's actually a terrible idea. It amounts to outsourcing U.S. tariff policy to other countries that they would dictate what our tariffs would be. Your reaction?

BESSENT: Look, I think that is a simplified reductionist view. It's typical of the old thinking, and it's the reason the U.S. has been taken advantage of, for years and years. It's the reason we have these gigantic trade deficits. It's the reason that many of our products aren't allowed — reasons that many of our products aren't allowed to be sold abroad. All President Trump wants is for American businesses to have frictionless trade, and I will stand behind our American manufacturers to make the best products at the best price and they can sell anywhere in the world without these barriers.

BARTIROMO: Let me move on to the amount of revenue you would expect, because the Committee for a Responsible Budget was out with some numbers in terms of what the tariffs on China would bring in, what the tariffs on the European Union would bring. Do you have an estimate in terms of what you're looking at, the kind of revenue you would expect from these tariffs?

BESSENT: Well, again, Maria, it's going to depend on our trading partners responses, how quickly they do it, how much they do it overtime. So, but I can tell you that these could be very, very substantial if they don't want to take their tariffs down because we will match them the percent for percent, dollar for dollar.

BARTIROMO: Let me get your take on sanctions, because Vice President J.D. Vance told The Wall Street Journal that the U.S. is considering new sanctions on Moscow and potential military action to push Vladimir Putin into a peace deal with Ukraine. He also said that the U.S. troops in Ukraine are not off the table. Zelenskyy told the Munich Security Conference he doesn't think the U.S. has a plan for peace in his country. He'll meet with Vance later today.

So, Mr. Secretary, what are the plans in terms of sanctions, which is another one of your roles at the Treasury, sanctions on Moscow? And then I want to get your take on the sanctions on Iran, because I know that the Biden administration blew off those sanctions, enabling Iran to raise billions by selling oil.

BESSENT: So, Maria, I was in Kyiv on Wednesday, met with President Zelenskyy and presented our vision, and President Trump's vision for a solution for this horrible war. And President Trump has what I think is one of the most elegant plans I've ever seen for ending this war. Part of it starts with intertwining the Ukraine — Ukrainian economy more with the U.S., and making sure that U.S. taxpayers, they receive the return for the money they put in, and then helping the Ukrainian people accelerate out — of this crisis after the war.

And the second part of this plan, if necessary, the Russian leadership should know that if President Trump directs me, we will go to maximum sanctions on Russian energy as soon as possible.

BARTIROMO: Yes, no, I'm going to get to that in a moment, because I love this idea of actually getting something for the money that we've been sending to Ukraine, and I know everyone appreciates the fact that you traveled all the way to Ukraine, so I'm going to get to that in a moment. Bottom line, are you expecting to put sanctions on Moscow?

BESSENT: Again, just like with the tariffs, it will depend on how we — how President Putin comes to the table. If we believe that taking the sanctions regime up to a maximum threshold level will help us achieve negotiating leverage. And as you know, no one understands negotiating leverage better than President Trump, so that will be his decision, and Treasury will implement it.

BARTIROMO: What about — so, I mean, the President said he was signing an executive order to return maximum pressure to Iran. Are you saying that you're not there yet?

BESSENT: Well, I'm sorry, I was talking about Russia. So with Iran, we are committed to bringing the Iranians to — going back to the 100,000 barrel a day of oil exports. When President Trump left office, that was the — they were at 100,000 barrels a day. Now they're 1.5 million, 1.6, million barrels a day, and that's how the Iranian regime is funding these terrorist activities around the world. The Chinese perhaps the Indians are buying the sanctioned Iranian oil, and that is unacceptable. So we think we can apply a maximum economic pressure to the Iranian regime.

And I will tell you I have seen the internal numbers, and their economy is quite fragile right now. They have massive inflation. They have a gigantic budget deficit, and that is with the $1.6 million — 1.6 million barrels a day of oil being sold. So if we get them back to the Trump 1.0 levels, I believe that they will be in severe economic distress.

BARTIROMO: Yes, and I guess that's the point, if they don't stop supporting these terrorist proxies. Let me go back to your meeting with Zelenskyy, sir. Mr. Secretary, you met with the head of Ukraine earlier this week. You traveled there to present an economic partnership agreement. This deal would trade access to Ukrainian natural resources, including those critical rare earth minerals for continued U.S. military support. Tell us about this. What is most attractive to the United States that Ukraine has, and is this basically the way that the U.S. will continue to support Ukraine?

BESSENT: Exactly, Maria, that we — we want the American people to understand that currently, we are aligned with Ukrainians on values. We are aligned with them on security, and we want to become more aligned with them on economics. And I think that is win-win. As the American people have a stake in the recovery of Ukrainian economy post this conflict, and we will get to the other side of this conflict, then the U.S. will share in the benefits, and by having U.S. partnership capital and ingenuity, I think Ukrainian economy that the U.S. involvement will act as an accelerant.

You know, just next door to Ukraine, is one of the great success stories of the past 30 years is Poland. Poland has had the second highest economic growth in the world since the 1990s after China. So why couldn't Ukraine do that? And why shouldn't the American people share in that through rare earths, through their energy and through their state owned enterprises? And we bring best practices in terms of privatization, in terms of modernization.

BARTIROMO: Did Zelenskyy agree with that, Secretary? Did you get a deal done?

BESSENT: President Zelenskyy is going to be discussing this today with Vice President Vance at the Munich Security Conference, and I am confident we will get a deal done.

BARTIROMO: And one of the rare earth minerals is titanium, which we need, by the way, for data centers and to power up our own plans for artificial intelligence, is that one of the attractive minerals that you referred to?

BESSENT: There is a whole array of strategic minerals that we have. And the other thing what we're going to have to do a lot of the processing you can have the minerals. A lot of the processing still takes place in China. So we're also going to be working on secure processing, either here in the U.S. or in Europe.

BARTIROMO: And Secretary, I want to get your take on cutting out waste, because, of course, DOGE agency is going to agency after agency trying to cut out wasteful spending. Are there also opportunities to do this at the Treasury?

BESSENT: Maria, I will tell you that this caricature that's being painted — created of DOGE is by the people who don't want accountability. And these are serious people, and they are going from agency to agency, doing an audit, looking for best practices. And what I have seen is that — at the agencies above us is where most of the problems are. And sure, at Treasury, we can always do a better job too, but that this fraud and abuse that's gone on. There's a lot of complaining now, I didn't see anyone complaining when the budget deficit was blown out. This money went flying out the door, flying out the door, just before the election, right after the election, and there's going to have to be some accountability.

BARTIROMO: And I want to get your take on what that means, practically speaking, in terms of agencies being shut down. Elon Musk spoke about it this week. Here he is from Dubai. Watch this.

(BEGIN VIDEO CLIP)

ELON MUSK, CEO, X: I think we do need to delete entire agencies as opposed to leave part of them behind. If you don't get remove the roots of the weed, then it's easy for the weed to grow back.

(END VIDEO CLIP)

BARTIROMO: Are you expecting entire agencies to go away with regard to financial services? I'm talking about, you know, whether or not the IRS merges into another agency, the CFTC merges into the Securities and Exchange Commission. What are the plans?

BESSENT: Look, we're examining everything. But again, kind of this hysteria that things are going to be merged, that things are going to be liquidated, is we are doing a thorough accounting, like I like to say, at Treasury, we move deliberately, and we fix things, and we are going to fix this, and if there's overlap — and as far as the IRS, with the IRS, I have three goals, it's collections, privacy and customer service. And I don't think there's anyone, anyone in the country, who thinks that they — that the IRS has achieved its potential in either of those three.

BARTIROMO: And one of the things that President Trump said was wasting a lot of money as is producing the penny. Does it really cost two cents to make one penny?

BESSENT: It actually cost a bit more than that. So we're going to stop producing the penny. And there are examples of this all over government where things — the machine just runs on until someone says, why, and it's cost cutter like President Trump with this DOGE. And remember, DOGE is the Department of Government Efficiency, so we want government to run better for the American taxpayer, and there are plenty of wonderful civil servants who come in every day and want to make things run better, and we want to partner with them and give them the means to do that.

BARTIROMO: Secretary, before you go, I just have one last question for you. The Committee for Responsible Budget says that President Trump's tariffs include a 10 percent additional tariff on all imports from China, and they say that tariff will raise $20 billion in revenue through the rest of this calendar year, and 200 billion through fiscal year 2035, do you agree with those numbers?

BESSENT: That's just for the China tariff, that could be approximately right. I haven't seen the exact numbers. We're still working on those.

BARTIROMO: Secretary, thank you so much.

BESSENT: Again, these are big numbers, Maria, so thanks.

BARTIROMO: Absolutely. And the president said it was staggering. We so appreciate your time. Secretary, we'll be watching your work, of course. Secretary Scott Bessent joining us. Thank you, sir.

BESSENT: Good to see you, Maria.

BARTIROMO: And to you.

END

data.title: Colombia Imports Increase 7.5% in December
data.description:
Imports to Colombia surged 7.5% year-on-year to USD 5.651 billion in December 2024, marking the eighth increase in the last twenty-six months, driven by higher purchases of manufactured products (8.7%) and extractive industries (14.7%).

Major import partners included the US (26.4% of total acquisitions), China (25.6%), and Mexico (4.8%).

data.title: Restaurant Brands International falls after TD Cowen downgrades to 'hold'
data.description:
** Shares of Burger King parent Restaurant Brands International , down about 3% in early trading

** TD Cowen downgrades stock to "hold" from "buy"

** Brokerage sees risks in Canada from immigration reform and tariffs weighing on Tim Horton chain's successful track record

** QSR beat Q4 results on Wednesday on demand for value meals at Burger King and strength of Tim Horton in Canada

** Brokerage sees a lack of confidence that Burger King has a compelling strategy to combat peers' chicken-focused menu innovation, while company looks to fill the CMO role

** 14 of 22 brokerages rate the stock "buy" or higher, seven "hold" and one "sell"; with a median PT of $78

** U.S.-listed shares fell 16.6% and Toronto shares fell 9.5% in 2024

data.title: European Equities Traded in the US as American Depositary Receipts Lower in Friday Trading, Up 1% for Week
data.description:
European equities traded in the US as American depositary receipts were trending modestly lower late Friday morning, down 0.23% to 1,399.64 on the S&P Europe Select ADR Index. Despite the decline, the index is still up about 1% for the week.

From continental Europe, the gainers were led by biopharmaceutical company Cellectis and financial services company ING Groep , which rose 5.4% and 1.7% respectively. They were followed by petroleum refiner Equinor and pharmaceutical company Ascendis Pharma , which were up 1.6% and 1.4% respectively.

The decliners from continental Europe were led by biopharmaceutical companies Genfit and argenx , which fell 5.5% and 2% respectively. They were followed by biotech company BioNTech and telecommunications operator Telefonica , which dropped 1.8% and 1.4% respectively.

From the UK and Ireland, the gainers were led by biotech company Autolus Therapeutics and pharmaceutical company Silence Therapeutics , which increased 6.2% and 5.8% respectively. They were followed by biopharmaceutical companies NuCana and Verona Pharma , which advanced 2.8% and 2.3% respectively.

The decliners from the UK and Ireland were led by biopharmaceutical companies TC Biopharm and Biodexa Pharmaceuticals , which lost 17% and 4.2% respectively. They were followed by tobacco company British American Tobacco and software development company Endava , which were down 1.5% and 1.4% respectively.

data.title: Spanish group from Basque Country agrees to buy stake in Talgo
data.description:
A Spanish consortium from the Basque Country, made up of shareholders in steel maker Sidenor, the regional government and local lender Kutxabank, has reached an agreement to buy a stake in train maker Talgo, local authorities said on Friday.

"The return of the decision-making centre to our territory means that we recover one of our industrial hallmarks," said Ramiro Gonzalez, the head of the Alava province, one of the Basque Country's three provinces.

No other details of the deal were immediately available although the consortium said last week it offered up to 4.80 euros ($4.95) per Talgo share, though part of that price - 0.65 euros - would be paid only if the company meets financial targets in 2027 and 2028.

The total offer implies a maximum valuation of about 595 million euros for the entire company.

data.title: Colombia Trade Gap Widens in December
data.description:
Colombia's trade deficit widened to USD 1.178 billion in December 2024, up from a USD 0.811 billion deficit in the same month last year.

The increase was driven by imports rising faster than exports.

Imports rose by 7.5% year-on-year to USD 5.651 billion, marking the eighth increase in the past 26 months, with higher purchases of manufactured products (8.7%) and extractive industries (14.7%).

Meanwhile, exports rose by a marginal 0.6% year-on-year to USD 4.472 billion, primarily led by a 29.5% surge in shipments from the agricultural industry, followed by the manufactured goods (16.5%).

However, fuels and extractive industries fell by 18.8%.

data.title: Evolent Health Price Target Maintained With a $12.00/Share by JMP Securities
M/I HOMES, INC. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
M/I Homes, Inc., a leading homebuilder in the United States, has released its 2024 Form 10-K report, showcasing a year of record financial performance and strategic growth initiatives. The company operates in the highly competitive homebuilding industry, focusing on delivering high-quality homes across various regions in the U.S. The report highlights significant financial achievements, operational milestones, strategic initiatives, and the challenges and risks faced by the company.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $4,504.7 million, reflecting a 12% increase from the previous year due to record homes delivered.
  • Gross Profit: $1,198.9 million, calculated as Total Revenue less Land and Housing Costs, with an improvement in gross margin percentage.
  • Operating Income: $706.1 million, an increase driven by higher home deliveries and improved construction cycle times.
  • Net Income: $563.7 million, marking a 21% increase from the prior year, setting a company record.
  • Diluted EPS: $19.71, up from $16.21 in the previous year, indicating strong earnings growth per share.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company operates in two main segments: Northern and Southern homebuilding regions, and financial services. The Northern region includes markets such as Chicago, Cincinnati, and Columbus, while the Southern region covers areas like Orlando, Tampa, and Austin. Financial services support homebuilding by providing mortgage loans and title services.
  • Geographical Performance: The Northern region saw a 25% increase in homebuilding revenue, driven by a 22% increase in homes delivered. The Southern region experienced a 3% increase in homebuilding revenue, attributed to a 5% increase in homes delivered. The Northern region's backlog decreased by 9%, while the Southern region's backlog decreased by 20%, reflecting improved construction cycle times.
  • Sales Units: The company delivered a record 9,055 homes in 2024, a 12% increase from the previous year. New contracts increased by 8% to 8,584, with an absorption pace of 3.3 sales per community per month. The average sales price of homes delivered remained at $483,000.
  • New Community Openings: In 2024, the company opened 72 new communities, with 21 in the Northern region and 51 in the Southern region. This expansion was partly due to prior year delays that were pushed to 2024.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to open additional new communities in 2025, increasing the average community count by approximately 5%. It expects to continue offering sales incentives and mortgage interest rate buydowns to support homebuyers and drive order activity. The company also anticipates some margin compression in 2025 due to current market conditions.
  • Operational Strategy: The company focuses on maximizing profitability, expanding market share through affordable and move-up product designs, and being selective in land and land development investments. It emphasizes managing land spend, construction cycle times, and maintaining a strong balance sheet.
  • Environmental and Social Initiatives: The company is committed to sustainable practices, including building energy-efficient homes and promoting diversity, equity, and inclusion within its workforce. It has established a DEI Committee and published an annual Environmental, Social, and Governance Report.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Community Expansion and Land Acquisition: M/I Homes, Inc. focused on expanding its community count and land acquisition to support future growth. The company opened 72 new communities in 2024 and plans to increase its average community count by approximately 5% in 2025. It invested $472.9 million in land acquisitions and $646.0 million in land development during 2024 to replenish its lot supply and support home construction.
  • Capital Management: The company maintained a strong balance sheet with $821.6 million in cash, cash equivalents, and restricted cash as of December 31, 2024. It repurchased $177.0 million of its outstanding common shares under the 2024 and 2021 Share Repurchase Programs, with authorization to repurchase an additional $106.7 million under the 2024 program. The company announced a new 2025 Share Repurchase Program authorizing up to $250 million in share repurchases. M/I Homes also managed its debt levels, with a homebuilding debt to capital ratio of 19% at year-end 2024, and maintained compliance with all covenants under its credit facilities and senior notes.
  • Future Outlook: Looking ahead, M/I Homes plans to continue its strategic focus on land acquisition and community development to support long-term growth. The company expects to selectively offer sales incentives in 2025 to support homebuyers and drive sales, while managing its land spend and inventory levels. It aims to maintain a strong balance sheet and liquidity, providing flexibility to navigate uncertain economic conditions. The company also plans to extend its MIF Mortgage Repurchase Facility before its expiration in October 2025, ensuring continued support for its mortgage financing operations.

Challenges and Risks

  • Market and Economic Risks: The homebuilding industry is cyclical and affected by changes in economic conditions, such as employment levels, interest rates, and consumer confidence. Increased mortgage interest rates have made it difficult for potential customers to qualify for financing, contributing to affordability issues.
  • Competitive Risks: M/I Homes faces competition from both public and private homebuilders, as well as community developers and land development companies. Increased competition could reduce new contracts and homes delivered.
  • Regulatory Risks: The company is subject to extensive government regulations, which could restrict business operations and increase costs. Regulatory changes, such as tax law changes and environmental regulations, could impact demand and increase operational costs.
  • Operational Risks: Supply shortages and labor demand could increase costs and delay deliveries. The company is also exposed to risks related to its indebtedness, which could restrict operations and affect financial performance.
  • Management Strategies: Management acknowledges the challenges posed by elevated inflation and mortgage interest rates, which have impacted affordability and demand. The company has used sales incentives and mortgage interest rate buydowns to stimulate demand. Management plans to increase land acquisition and development investment activity in 2025 to support future growth, while managing land spend and inventory levels. The company remains focused on controlling overhead leverage and offering homebuyer incentives to navigate evolving market conditions.

SEC Filing: M/I HOMES, INC. [ MHO ] - 10-K - Feb. 14, 2025

data.title: Ben & Jerry's says parent Unilever mandating silence on Trump
data.description:
Ben & Jerry's accused its parent Unilever of demanding its silence toward U.S. President Donald Trump, as it prepares to spin off the ice cream brand later this year.

In a Thursday night filing in Manhattan federal court, Ben & Jerry's said Unilever ice cream chief Peter ter Kulve earlier in the day unilaterally banned it from "issuing any posts criticizing President Trump" pending further review.

Ben & Jerry's said ter Kulve cited Unilever's restructuring in defending his actions, saying it created a "new dynamic" in an "unprecedented time."

Ter Kulve previously banned a planned post on hot-button issues when Trump was inaugurated on January 20 because the post mentioned Trump, Ben & Jerry's has said.

Unilever did not immediately respond on Friday to requests for comment. Ben & Jerry's did not immediately respond to a similar request.

Ben & Jerry's accusations came in its lawsuit seeking to stop Unilever's alleged efforts to dismantle its independent board and end its social activism.

Many companies have altered social policies, including on diversity, or appeared to show deference toward Trump since he began his second White House term.

Ben & Jerry's has had a socially conscious mission since being founded by Ben Cohen and Jerry Greenfield in 1978.

Unilever bought Ben & Jerry's in 2000. They have battled publicly since 2021, when Ben & Jerry's halted sales in the Israeli-occupied West Bank. Its business there was later sold.

The planned spinoff comes as Unilever simplifies its product portfolio, which has dozens of brands including Dove, Hellmann's, Knorr, Surf and Vaseline.

Unilever on Thursday chose Amsterdam over London and New York for the primary listing when it spins off Ben & Jerry's, Breyers, Magnum and other ice cream brands as a standalone company. Ice cream revenue totaled 8.3 billion euros ($8.72 billion) in 2024.

The case is Ben & Jerry's Homemade Inc v Unilever et al, U.S. District Court, Southern District of New York, No. 24-08641.

($1 = 0.9519 euros)

data.title: Intel shares slip, but on pace for best week in over two decades
data.description:
** Shares of Intel fall 4.6% to $23.02, set to snap a four-day winning streak

** However, shares are on pace for weekly gains of over 20%; the best week since January, 2000

** White House is seeking to renegotiate U.S. CHIPS and Science Act awards and has signaled delays to some upcoming disbursements, Reuters reports

** Weekly gains are likely due to investors buying INTC on hopes that sector heavyweight Nvidia's earnings, due February 26, will lift other chip stocks - Larry Tentarelli, chief technical strategist, Blue Chip Daily Trend Report

** "(Intel) got oversold ... quite a bit after the DeepSeek news ... what you've got is traders buying ahead of earnings, hoping that Nvidia will beat earnings like they normally do" - Tentarelli

** INTC is the worst-performing component of the Philadelphia SE Semiconductor Index over the past one year, down over 47% versus the index's 13% gain

** Intel also lifted earlier in the week after U.S. Vice President JD Vance said Donald Trump's administration would ensure the most powerful artificial intelligence systems would be built in the country

** Including today's moves, Intel is up 14.2% this year versus the SOX's 2.7% rise

data.title: UK's Segro plans data centre strategy shift as AI booms
data.description:
Segro , which has historically offered data centres equipped only with power connections, plans to develop full-fledged facilities to directly serve major cloud providers like Amazon, Microsoft , and Alphabet's Google in a bid to shore up rental income.

The largest-listed European property company is looking to capitalise the surge in demand for data centres, a trend fuelled by the growing reliance on AI systems, which require specialised infrastructure like high capacity power supply and advanced cooling systems.

Typically, Segro leases out data centres to firms that add infrastructure such as chillers, generators, and dividing walls before sub-leasing to tech companies, which then add their own technology.

Under the new strategy, the London-based company will lease data centres fitted with required infrastructure directly to end users.

"A 'powered shell' might cost 50 million pounds ($62.9 million) to build, whereas a 'fully fitted' data centre could require around 500 million pounds. However, rental income could jump from 5 million pounds to 50 million pounds," Segro CFO Soumen Das told Reuters.

Segro currently earns about 650 million pounds in annual rent from its broader portfolio, and Das added that a single fully fitted data centre would have a noticeable impact on overall revenue.

The group, which mainly owns big box and urban warehouses among other assets, has 34 'powered shells', all in London and Slough, accounting for 8% of its overall portfolio.

The London- and Paris-listed firm said it is planning for its new data centres to also incorporate 'fully fitted' facilities, but did not provide additional details or a timeline.

However the new strategy comes with a trade-off, with Das saying fully fitted spaces would depreciate faster than the 'powered shells', potentially impacting long-term performance.

($1 = 0.7948 pounds)

data.title: CDC to lose one-tenth of workforce under Trump administration probationary job cuts - AP
data.description: Nearly 1,300 probationary employees at the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention are being forced out under the Trump administration's move to get rid of all probationary employees, the Associated Press reported on Friday, citing a federal official.

data.title: Alamos Gold Keeps Outperform Rating at RBC Capital Markets, National Bank of Canada
data.description:
Alamos Gold kept its stock rating and price target at RBC Capital Markets and National Bank of Canada, according to separate notes published Thursday.

RBC retained Alamos' Outperform rating and US$27 price target

RBC's view was "positive". It said while long-dated (with production planned in about 2040), adding higher margin mine life to the Lynn Lake project (about 10% of NAV) is "nominally positive and should improve overall economics by deferring lower-grade stockpile production".

Investors, the bank added, should focus on growth expected after 2026 with full production from Island in 2027 and Lynn Lake in 2029 leading to lower costs and higher production, which RBC now forecasts reaching about 1moz by 2029.

"While the stock continues to trade at a premium to peers during the investment period at Island, Mulatos (PDA), and now Lynn Lake, we see AGI as well positioned to grow, with a

high quality portfolio of long-life assets primarily in Canada justifying the valuation vs. intermediate peers.

Meanwhile, Alamos maintained its Outperform rating and C$38 price target at National Bank of Canada, which noted that the results demonstrate the strong production growth potential within the company's portfolio.

The bank said the Outperform rating is based on Alamos having one of the best potential NAV/sh growth profiles in the sector, which supports the potential to set new 52-week highs as EBITDA should continue to grow.

The price target was derived from a 100% 10.5x NTM EV/EBITDA target multiple, which implies a P/NAV of 1.30x.

"Alamos also benefits from a portfolio heavily weighted to Canada, further supporting a premium valuation vs. its peers," National Bank said. "Thus, we continue to view Alamos Gold as one of the best ideas for a medium-term investment period."

Alamos' share price was down 2.7% at last look to $31.86 on the TSX.

data.title: Crypto scams likely set new record in 2024 helped by AI, Chainalysis says
data.description:
The rise of "pig butchering" scams and the increasing use of generative artificial intelligence likely lifted revenues from crypto scams to a record high in 2024, according to blockchain analytics firm Chainalysis.

Revenue from pig butchering scams, where perpetrators cultivate relationships with individuals and convince them to participate in fraudulent schemes, increased nearly 40% in 2024 from the previous year, the firm estimated in a report published on Thursday.

Revenue in 2024 from crypto scams was at least $9.9 billion, although the figure could rise to a record high of $12.4 billion once more data becomes available, it said.

"Crypto fraud and scams have continued to increase in sophistication," Chainalysis researchers said.

The company pointed to marketplaces that support pig butchering operations and the use of GenAI as factors making it easier and cheaper for scammers to expand operations.

Indeed, GenAI technology could potentially "exponentially scale crypto scams", Chainalysis said.

The company, which tracks publicly available transaction data on the blockchain to identify scam revenue, said crypto fraud activity grew 24% each year on average since 2020.

Cryptocurrencies, most notably bitcoin, have soared in price and popularity over the past few years as investors chased banner returns and interest in blockchain technology soared.

The sector has jumped significantly since U.S. President Donald Trump's victory in the November election on hopes of an easier regulatory environment.

Other particularly lucrative scams included crypto drainers, where scammers pose as blockchain projects and take control of victims' crypto wallets, and high-yield investment scams that promised outsized returns, according to Chainalysis.

In January 2024, a crypto drainer posed as the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission after the regulator's X account was compromised.

Cryptocurrency ATMs have also been key hotspots for scams, according to Chainalysis, with perpetrators often impersonating government officials or customer support agents to convince victims to deposit cash into the machines.

data.title: Lottery.com to Acquire Technology to Support International Expansion
data.description:
Lottery.com said Friday it has signed a memorandum of understanding with PlusEVO to buy an advanced lottery management system and gaming platform to support its international expansion.

The all-stock purchase is valued at $1.5 million, payable over 30 months, with shares priced at $3 each, the company said.

The planned acquisition supports Lottery.com's goal to generate international revenue by March 31 through its technology stack, the company said.

Shares of the company were up more than 22% in recent trading.
